'''Lloyd White''' (born 9 August 1988) is a Welsh international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He is now head coach of [[Wales A national rugby league team|Wales A]]

He has previously played for [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]] and spent time playing for the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Friendly Preview: South Wales vs Wigan">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=35406 |title=Friendly Preview: South Wales vs Wigan |publisher=rleague.com |date=2010-02-22 |access-date=2010-02-22 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150120095012/http://rleague.com/db/article.php?id=35406 |archive-date=20 January 2015 }}</ref> He played as a {{rlp|so}} earlier in his career.<ref name="Lloyd White Profile @ rleague.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/w/white_lloyd/index.php|title=Lloyd White Profile @ rleague.com|publisher=rleague.com|date=2009-12-17|access-date=2009-12-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130206091118/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/w/white_lloyd/index.php|archive-date=6 February 2013|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Background==
White was born in Cardiff, Wales. He is of Jamaican heritage.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lloyd-white-named-in-jamaica-world-cup-train-on-squad/|title=Lloyd White named in Jamaica World Cup train-on squad|first=Josh|last=McAllister|date=17 June 2021|website=LoveRugbyLeague}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Crusaders===
[[File:Lloyd White.JPG|thumb|right|White playing for the Crusaders against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2009]]
White made six appearances for Crusaders in his début season of 2009.<ref name="Crusaders 2009 Stats">{{cite web|url=http://www.crusadersrfl.com/2009stats.htm|title=Crusaders 2009 Stats|publisher=crusadersrfl.com|date=2009-12-17|access-date=2009-12-17|url-status=usurped|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091123111901/http://www.crusadersrfl.com/2009stats.htm|archive-date=23 November 2009 }}</ref> Following the demise of Crusaders, White joined [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], starting in 2012.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40642 Mellars makes Wildcats move] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111014041043/http://rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40642 |date=14 October 2011 }} ''rleague.com'', 1 August 2011</ref>

===South Wales Scorpions===
He spent some time on loan at the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]] in 2010.

===Widnes Vikings===
On 1 May 2014, White committed to the Vikings on a new two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30113/white-commits-to-widnes |title=White commits to Widnes |website=www.superleague.co.uk |access-date=17 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140502045444/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30113/white-commits-to-widnes |archive-date=2 May 2014 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/9291704/lloyd-white-becomes-latest-player-to-pledge-his-future-to-widnes-vikings|title=Lloyd White becomes latest player to pledge his future to Widnes Vikings|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 5 November 2019, Toulouse announced that they had signed Lloyd White from Australian side Mackay Cutters for the 2020 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/un-2eme-international-gallois-rejoint-le-to-xiii/|title=Un 2ème International Gallois rejoint le TO XIII|website=To13.com|date=5 November 2019|access-date=28 October 2021}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/50305672|title=Wales' White signs for Toulouse|access-date=28 October 2021|website=Bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In June 2020, shortly before the 2020 Championship season was abandoned due to Covid 19, White announced that he had extended his contract with Toulouse for the 2021 season with an option for 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lloyd-white-signs-new-deal-with-toulouse/|title=Lloyd White signs new deal with Toulouse|first=Drew|last=Darbyshire|date=17 June 2020|website=LoveRugbyLeague.com|access-date=28 October 2021}}</ref>

On 14 October 2021, Toulouse announced that White had extended his contract until the end of the 2022 season.<ref name="auto1">{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/lhistoire-continue-entre-le-to-et-lloyd-white/|title=L'histoire continue entre le TO et Lloyd WHITE|website=To13.com|date=19 October 2021|access-date=28 October 2021}}</ref>

==International career==
White has represented his native [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international level in 2009, making his début in the 42-12 win over [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[European Cup (rugby league)|European Cup]],<ref name="Wales 42-12 Ireland">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1236|title=Wales 42-12 Ireland|publisher=rlef.eu.com|date=2009-12-17|access-date=2009-12-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111116025514/http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1236|archive-date=16 November 2011|url-status=dead}}</ref> and going on to play in the final winning team against [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], scoring a try in a 28-16 win.<ref name="European Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1259|title=European Cup Final|publisher=rlef.eu.com|date=2009-12-16|access-date=2009-12-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111116024154/http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1259|archive-date=16 November 2011|url-status=dead}}</ref>

White was named the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] player of the year in 2011 at the RLIF awards.<ref>{{cite news|title=Slater scoops player award|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15313/7289077/Slater-scoops-player-award|access-date=5 November 2011|newspaper=[[Sky Sports]]|date=3 November 2011}}</ref>

In September 2015, [[John Kear]] named White as the Wales national team captain for the [[2015 European Cup]] tournament.<ref>{{cite news
| title = WALES NAME CAPTAIN FOR RUGBY LEAGUE EUROPEAN CHAMPIONSHIP
| publisher = rlef.eu.com
| date = 2015-09-16
| url = http://www.rlef.eu.com/news/article/1128/wales-name-captain-for-rugby
| access-date = 2015-09-17
| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20150928172642/http://www.rlef.eu.com/news/article/1128/wales-name-captain-for-rugby
| archive-date = 28 September 2015
| url-status = dead
}}</ref> On 12 October, however, he required surgery on an injured knee so he therefore withdrew from the squad.<ref>{{cite news 
| title = Wigan's Ben Flower could captain Wales in European Championship
| publisher = Sky Sports
| date = 2015-10-12
| url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10026262/wigans-ben-flower-could-captain-wales-in-european-championship
| format = 
| access-date = 2015-10-13}}</ref>

In October 2016, White played for Wales in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe|2017 World Cup qualifiers]].

He was selected in the [[Wales 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|date=9 October 2019|title=16-man squad for 9s World Cup revealed|url=https://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16722/-man-squad-for-s-world-cup-revealed|publisher=Wales RL|access-date=1 November 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191010051335/https://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16722/-man-squad-for-s-world-cup-revealed|archive-date=10 October 2019|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2021, White was named in the train-on squad for  the [[Jamaica national rugby league team]] ahead of the 2021 Rugby League World Cup.<ref name="auto"/>

==Coaching career==
On 2 October 2025 he was announced as head-coach of the [[Wales A national rugby league team|Wales 'A']] side<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=2 Oct 2025|accessdate=3 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League stalwart lands international coaching role as assistant confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lloyd-white-lands-international-coaching-role}}</ref>
